Identity and scope are consistent across the registry binding (SEARCH_CORE_CP244_PACK_BINDING), manifest, contract current_pack + appended historical_pack_bindings tail, validator script, and model tests. Deliverable distribution {security_audit:1, implementation:5, ui:3, claude_review:1} and micro-title {Synthetic Fixture Set:10} match the 10 manifest units exactly and are enforced by validateSearchCoreCp244Coverage. Descriptor-only and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced redundantly: freezeCp244Result forces all dispatch/write/persist/lock/audit/receipt flags false; validateSearchCoreCp244ServiceFixtureMidDescriptor asserts per-row descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false, plus targeted no-leak checks for audit hint/body, state-transition writes, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, db-row creation, validation-error detail, and review/approval route dispatch; and the contract projection is held to deep-equality with the freshly generated descriptor. Authority boundary is correctly preserved (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false); Hermes appears only as a deterministic packet/receipt-shape descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. production_ready is correctly false with status implemented_pending_review, so no premature enterprise-trust claim is made.
